tools.name: search_events_tool
tools.description:
Return individual raw honeypot events with all fields. Use when the user wants to see
actual records: 'show me events from this IP', 'what hit port 443 last week', 'events from
Russia yesterday'. Filters: source_ip, country (2-letter code), asn (e.g. 'AS12345'),
dest_port, protocol ('tls' or '' = th

tools.name: top_attackers_tool
tools.description:
Ranked leaderboard of attack sources. Use for: 'who is attacking the most?', 'top
attacking countries', 'most targeted ports', 'most common user agents', 'top ASNs by
attack volume', 'top IPs from China', 'top attackers hitting port 22'.
'by' controls grouping: ip, asn, country, port, user_agent, ja

tools.name: ioc_lookup_tool
tools.description:
Look up any IP address or domain in the honeypot dataset. Use this FIRST whenever the
user asks: 'is this IP malicious?', 'is this a known scanner?', 'have you seen this IP?',
'what does this IP do?', 'when was it last seen?', 'is this IP in your data?'. Returns:
total_events (0 = never observed), f

tools.name: payload_search_tool
tools.description:
Full-text search across HTTP URL paths and user agents in attack traffic. Use for:
'find attacks targeting /wp-admin', 'show exploit attempts for CVE-2024-XXXX', 'find
requests with this user agent string', 'what payloads hit port 80 last week'. Pro/Team
plan only. since/until are ISO-8601 UTC strin

tools.name: attack_timeline_tool
tools.description:
Attack volume over time, bucketed by hour or day. Use for: 'show attack trends this
week', 'was there a spike on port 22?', 'how has SSH scanning changed?', 'attack volume
from China over 30 days'. bucket: 'hour' or 'day'. Optional filters: filter_protocol
('tls'/'''), filter_country (2-letter code)

tools.name: asn_enrich_tool
tools.description:
Full honeypot profile for an ASN (autonomous system / hosting provider). Use for:
'tell me about AS202425', 'what is Vultr doing in my honeypots?', 'attacks from this
hosting provider', 'attribute this IP to its network'. asn format: 'AS12345'.
Returns: total events, unique IPs, top targeted ports,

tools.name: fingerprint_search_tool
tools.description:
Search honeypot activity by TLS, HTTP, or SSH fingerprint. Use when a user asks:
'have you seen this JA4 fingerprint?', 'which IPs share this TLS fingerprint?', 'how
common is this HASSH?', 'find all scanners with this SSH client fingerprint'. fp_type:
'ja4' (TLS client), 'ja3' (legacy TLS client, M

tools.name: fingerprint_population_tool
tools.description:
The population behind a single client fingerprint: how many source IPs carry it,
across how many networks (ASNs) and countries, the ports they hit, the top networks
and a sample of the IPs, plus a read on whether it is concentrated (a likely
coordinated operation, many IPs on few networks) or spread

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: payload_search_tool, fingerprint_search_tool · Private data: search_events_tool, ioc_lookup_tool, attack_timeline_tool, fingerprint_search_tool, fingerprint_population_tool · Public sink: search_events_tool, fingerprint_search_tool · Destructive: asn_enrich_tool.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: payload_search_tool, fingerprint_search_tool · Private data: search_events_tool, ioc_lookup_tool, attack_timeline_tool, fingerprint_search_tool, fingerprint_population_tool · Public sink: search_events_tool, fingerprint_search_tool · Destructive: asn_enrich_tool

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

title: Tool description changed since last scan
detail: Tool "search_events_tool" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet:
before: "(hash 2ce100b4)" → after: "Return individual raw honeypot events with all fields. Use when the user wants to see
actual records: 'show me events from this IP', 'what hit port 443 last week', 'events from
Russia yesterday'. Filters: source_ip, country (2-letter code), asn (e.g. 'AS12345'),
dest_port, protocol ('tls' or '' = th"

remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
